spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the full round-trip the relaxation enables: createElement with Unicode tag, append, CSS.escape, querySelector, match. Spec-accurate.

interactive.rationale: Native-support probe, sandbox with editable tag + attribute inputs, nine-tile escape gallery (each clickable), and a separate attribute-selector sandbox — all invoke real DOM APIs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'gallery of 9 character sets … with live create/querySelector results and an attribute selector sandbox' — page delivers that count and shape.

edge_cases.rationale: Gallery covers nine scripts; never shows what CSS.escape does to leading digits, to whitespace inside names, or what happens when the same tag is appended twice and querySelector returns the first.

references.rationale: See-also section links only to the three sibling concept pages — no direct https://chromestatus.com/feature/6278918763708416 link. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires the chromestatus link on every concept page for self-heal.

design_system.rationale: Most colours from CSS variables; `.result-box.ok { background: #edfdf0 }` and `.result-box.err { background: #fde8e8 }` are raw hex backgrounds outside the documented token set.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Every concept must include https://chromestatus.com/feature/6278918763708416 directly per CLAUDE.md rule #3 (self-heal anchor for the slug-recovery tooling). Currently only the parent feature page carries it.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex on .result-box.ok / .result-box.err
openQuestions.detail: Use `var(--accent-emerald)` and `var(--accent-rose)` directly (with --text-black foreground for AA) instead of `#edfdf0` and `#fde8e8` so the design-system tokens stay consistent.

openQuestions.title: Show the CSS.escape-of-a-leading-digit case
openQuestions.detail: `CSS.escape('1foo')` returns `\31 foo` — a non-trivial transformation that the gallery never demonstrates. Adding `1abc` (which createElement still rejects) and `abc1` (which it accepts) as gallery tiles would show the asymmetry between what createElement accepts and what CSS.escape can handle.

openQuestions.title: Test querySelectorAll with duplicate Unicode tags
openQuestions.detail: Appending two `<artículo>` elements and using querySelectorAll(CSS.escape('artículo')) would surface the case-sensitivity and selector-list behaviour the spec preserves. Currently the sandbox only ever creates one element so there's no ambiguity to resolve.

summary: Strong full round-trip story: create + escape + select + verify. Two real fixes — missing chromestatus link (CLAUDE.md rule #3) and raw-hex result-box backgrounds — plus a couple of edge-case galleries would round it out.
